control_description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

evidence_files.description: GCP service account configuration and key status
CISO-approved security exceptions with compensating controls

noise_files.description: BCP executive summary — not relevant to CC6.1
Vendor risk assessments — not relevant to CC6.1

prompt: You are conducting a SOC 2 Type II readiness assessment for Vantage Corp covering Q4 2025 (October 1 – December 31, 2025). You have been provided with multiple evidence artifacts. Review all documents, cross-reference them, and assess compliance with CC6.1 (Logical Access Security). Note that not all documents may be relevant to this control. Identify genuine findings only — consider whether exceptions or compensating controls adequately address apparent gaps before flagging them.

findings.title: Legacy ETL service account has a manual key (but has valid CISO exception)
findings.description: The legacy-etl service account has a manually created key, violating the policy's workload identity requirement. However, the exception register shows EXC-2025-004 — a current, CISO-approved exception with documented compensating controls (IP allowlisting, Vault storage, daily audit). A competent auditor would note the exception and move on, not flag this as a finding.
findings.expected_reasoning: Initial red flag: manual key exists → check exception register → EXC-2025-004 is active and approved → compensating controls are documented → not a finding.

findings.title: Terraform import service account with editor role still active 9 months after use
findings.description: terraform-import has roles/editor (extremely broad permissions) and a key that was last used March 12, 2025. The exception register shows EXC-2025-003 expired on April 8, 2025. The key and service account were supposed to be deleted after the one-time import, but they are still active 9 months later. The exception has expired, so this is no longer covered.
findings.expected_reasoning: Service account has roles/editor → exception existed but expired April 2025 → key still exists and was never deleted → exception no longer provides cover → stale privileged credential with broad access.

findings.title: Monitoring agent exception expired but key still in use
findings.description: The monitoring-agent service account has a manually created key (violating workload identity policy). Exception EXC-2025-002 covered this until December 10, 2025 — but the key was still actively used on December 28, 2025. The exception expired without renewal, so this service account is now operating outside the approved exception framework.
findings.expected_reasoning: Key actively used after exception expiration date → no current exception covers this → operating outside policy without approval.

findings.title: Legacy ETL exception compensating control (key rotation) not evidenced
findings.description: EXC-2025-001 and its renewal EXC-2025-004 list compensating controls including 'key stored in HashiCorp Vault' and 'automated daily key usage audit.' However, the GCP service account data shows the legacy-etl key was created on 2023-01-15 and never rotated (no rotation evident in the key data). If the compensating controls aren't actually being followed, the exception's risk acceptance is undermined.
findings.expected_reasoning: Exception is valid, but compensating controls claim rotation/audit → key dates show no rotation since 2023 → are the compensating controls actually operating? This is a control effectiveness question.
